commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a safe-integer guard to `parseByteSize` and tests two unsafe byte-size strings that currently round to corrupted finite numbers.

PR surface: Source +6, Tests +7. Total +13 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main's `parseByteSize` rounds and only checks `Number.isFinite`, and a read-only Node probe confirms the PR examples are finite but not safe integers; I did not run the PR test command because the checkout was kept read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can preserve the PR's parser fix while adding the missing safe-integer check and regression coverage for numeric byte-size config.

target.title: fix(cli): reject finite-but-unsafe byte sizes
target.body:
## Summary

`parseByteSize` (`src/cli/parse-bytes.ts`) computed `Math.round(value * multiplier)` and guarded only with `if (!Number.isFinite(bytes))`. For inputs above `Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER` the result is **finite but not a safe integer**, so the guard passed and the function silently returned a precision-corrupted value — e.g. `parseByteSize("9007199254740993")` returned `9007199254740992` (a different number than typed) and `parseByteSize("9000000tb")` returned `9895604649984000000`.

Its direct sibling `parseDurationMs` (`src/cli/parse-duration.ts`) was changed in commit `35cdd40182` ("fix(cli): reject unsafe duration values") from the identical `Number.isFinite` check to a `roundSafeDurationMs` helper that throws on `!Number.isSafeInteger`. `parse-bytes.ts` is the twin left on the old, now-known-buggy contract. `parseByteSize` is consumed on config-validation/runtime paths (`config/zod-schema.ts` runLog.maxBytes, session maxDiskBytes/highWaterBytes, `cron/run-log.ts`).

### Changes
- `parse-bytes.ts` — add `roundSafeByteSize(raw, value)` that throws via the existing `invalidByteSize` when the rounded result is not a safe integer, mirroring `roundSafeDurationMs`. `Number.isSafeInteger` also covers the non-finite case the old guard handled.
- `cli-utils.test.ts` — finite-but-unsafe cases.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: byte sizes above `Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER` were accepted and returned precision-corrupted instead of being rejected. After the fix they throw; valid sizes are unchanged.

Real environment tested: Node 22.22.1, macOS, no network/model. Vitest exercises the real exported `parseByteSize`. BEFORE is `origin/main`'s file (swapped via `git show`).

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/cli-utils.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix:
```
parseByteSize("9007199254740993")  BEFORE: 9007199254740992 (corrupted)   AFTER: throws "Invalid byte size"
parseByteSize("9000000tb")         BEFORE: 9895604649984000000 (corrupted) AFTER: throws "Invalid byte size"
parseByteSize("10mb")              BOTH: 10485760  (valid, unchanged)
```

Observed result after fix: out-of-safe-range sizes are rejected; all existing valid/invalid cases are unchanged. The new tests fail on `origin/main` and pass after.

What was not tested: end-to-end config validation (the test drives the real exported `parseByteSize` those schemas call).

## Additional checks
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/cli-utils.test.ts` passes; fail-before verified by swapping in `origin/main`'s file. `oxlint --tsconfig` clean.
target.author: ly-wang19

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
